title: Matelea oblongata
taxonomy: Matelea oblongata is classified within the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This classification places Matelea oblongata in the order Gentianales, a large order of flowering plants. The Apocynaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, including the Asclepiadoideae subfamily, which includes Matelea oblongata. As a member of the Asclepiadoideae subfamily, Matelea oblongata is closely related to other plants in this group. The exact classification of Matelea oblongata within the family Apocynaceae may be subject to change as new research and evidence become available.

intro: Matelea oblongata, also known as the "monkey's coat" or "monkey jacket", is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it beneficial for treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Matelea oblongata include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Matelea oblongata is traditionally used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and skin irritations.
